body: I think you could have used PagedAllocator which is basically a pool you can reuse.

body:

```suggestion
void GodotConcavePolygonShape3D::_fill_bvh(_Volume_BVH *p_bvh_tree, BVH *p_bvh_array, int &p_idx, PagedAllocator<_Volume_BVH, false> &r_pool) {
```

(`p_` prefix for parameters we only read, `r_` prefix for the ones we actually mutate)

body:
Tested locally (rebased on top of `master` 8e0346badefcd69656e1ae3ba12d6eaafd7a4fae), it works as expected.

On a release export template build in the testing project:

- Before: ~18000 µs for initial generation, 470 µs on average for subsequent generations
- After: ~18000 µs for initial generation, 330 µs on average for subsequent generations

Seems to fail because of missing space?

```suggestion
	Ref<OpenXRAction> ##varname;                                    \
```

BTW we usually name our macros in uppercase.
author: akien-mga

body: That space was removed by the clang-format I think... :(
author: BastiaanOlij

body:
Indeed, what a mess. That should work (also moved it in scope because I didn't see a reason for it to be out?):
```diff
diff --git a/modules/openxr/openxr_action_sets.cpp b/modules/openxr/openxr_action_sets.cpp
index 9d6e23ae56..867f5547cf 100644
--- a/modules/openxr/openxr_action_sets.cpp
+++ b/modules/openxr/openxr_action_sets.cpp
@@ -152,9 +152,10 @@ void OpenXRActionSets::create_default_action_sets() {
 	add_action_set(action_set);
 
 	// Create our actions
+	// clang-format off
 #define add_action(varname, actionname, actiontype, toplevel_paths) \
-	Ref<OpenXRAction>##varname;                                     \
 	{                                                               \
+		Ref<OpenXRAction> ##varname;                                \
 		##varname.instantiate();                                    \
 		##varname->set_name(String(#varname));                      \
 		##varname->set_localised_name(String(actionname));          \
@@ -162,6 +163,7 @@ void OpenXRActionSets::create_default_action_sets() {
 		##varname->parse_toplevel_paths(String(toplevel_paths));    \
 		action_set->add_action(##varname);                          \
 	}
+	// clang-format on
 
 	add_action(trigger, "Trigger", OpenXRAction::OPENXR_ACTION_FLOAT, "/user/hand/left,/user/hand/right");
 	add_action(trigger_click, "Trigger Click", OpenXRAction::OPENXR_ACTION_BOOL, "/user/hand/left,/user/hand/right");
```
author: akien-mga

body:
How complex would it be to turn `OpenXRDevice` into an interface and have a `dummy` and a `concrete` implementation similar to the pattern used in other areas?
That'll ensure the dummy version stays up-to-date as this class grows in functionality.
author: m4gr3d

body: @BastiaanOlij @akien-mga Is it possible to leverage `clang-format` to ensure this is properly observed?
author: m4gr3d

body:
`clang-format` is only a formatting tool, it can't change code.

`clang-tidy` can do code analysis and changes, including giving a default initialization to uninitialized variables, though it can be somewhat unwieldy to use (applies to the whole case, has false positives and creates compilation bugs).

But what's this C++20 notation that we can't use here? If it's `{}`, AFAIK it's available in C++17, we use it in some places.
author: akien-mga

body: It may be useful to move the above functions into a util header file to keep this class smaller and concise.

body: Yup, just a stub and I might comment it out if we end up merging the PR as is, but there are various places in the code where I've made preparations.
author: BastiaanOlij

body: Shall do :)
author: BastiaanOlij

body:
My main reason for not doing this is that the implementation is purely based on OpenXR, there really isn't a way to abstract things to some sort of template class that makes a lot of sense and especially for the vulkan extension, we'd be implementing far more on the dummy class while it is never even instantiated (the whole point is that if OpenXR is not available or not running, there will never be an instance).

This really is uniquely OpenXR and frankly frankenstein enough, while I understand why OpenXR went down this path, I really don't think they've architectures this very nicely. From a principled view they way OpenXR sets its hooks into Vulkan only makes sense for a purpose build XR engine.
author: BastiaanOlij

author: BastiaanOlij

body:
> The init with `{}` should zero everything in the struct and then you can set the members you care about outside the init.

We found out the hard way (note Godot crashing hard), that this is not true on every platform. Android did not zero out things when initialized this way and it ended up crashing stuff when the next pointer had a random value instead of being zero.
author: BastiaanOlij

body:
We should apply defensive programming here and elsewhere we use this pattern; we should check if the returned pointer is non-null before overwriting `next_pointer`.
If it's `null` we reuse the previous `next_pointer` value for the next invocation.

body: Is "kebab-case" (default) the default for filenames? I thought it was snake_case?
author: fire

body:
Right now, the default is kebab-case and it replaces underscores: **`t e_s-t` → `t-e-s-t`**
It would probably be better to keep underscores instead: **`t e_s-t` → `t-e_s-t`**

Could be implemented like this: `dir_name = project_name.to_lower().replace(" ", "-")`
author: nathanfranke

body: The default (kebab-case) is now implemented as `to_lower().replace(" ", "-")`. So underscores are preserved: **`t e_s-t` → `t-e_s-t`**

body:
Tested locally, looks pretty good! It's a great usability improvement, I really disliked how this worked until now.

The code changes are pretty hard to review in depth, for future PRs please try to keep refactoring separate from feature work. There's a lot of changes here which are just moving things around or slightly improving code quality which make the functional changes challenging to review. But overall it seems ok to me.

body:
I was unsure about adding this originally as creating new lines unlike `backspace` and `handle_unicode_input` are not called internally, so you could override the 3 actions (`ui_text_newline_above, ui_text_newline_blank, ui_text_newline`) in `gdscript` as appropriate and it should "just" work. However, if there is something not covered here, I have no issues with exposing it.

As for this code review, we should expose `new_line` with the two args or add additional callbacks for them.

body:
This is C# 10 syntax and the .csproj that we create by default targets `net472` (which defaults to C# 7.3) so it wouldn't really be valid but I think it's fine for this parser to consider it valid since it's only used to retrieve the script metadata.

In `master` this parser no longer exists as it was replaced with source generators (#46713).
